author: bharath.vedartham
content:
In ePBS, a builder should ideally reveal their payload only if the block which includes their bid gains enough attestations. 
From a technical standpoint, what is the easiest way to check within a slot if a proposed block has gained a certain threshold of attestations? 
I can think of 2 ways:
1. Listen to the Attestation SSE event via the beacon api, track the attestations received for the particular slot and do the book-keeping on the weight a block has received.
2. Directly subscribe to the gossipsub topic and track them in a similar way. Although we would have to do ssz decoding here I believe. 
Are there any other ways to do this? Even if we do the necessary book-keeping of attestations, it seems harder to account for proposer boost.
